story: Josephine Butler roared onto the scene in 1828, born on April 13 in Milfield, England. This fierce feminist became a powerful voice for change. She bravely fought against unfair laws and the terrible slave trade. Butler's passion for justice inspired many to join her cause, making the world a bit fairer for everyone.
activity_question: What did Josephine Butler fight against?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important to stand up for what's right, even when it's difficult?

title: Nobel Prize Geneticist Born in Brooklyn
story: A brilliant mind entered the world in Brooklyn, New York. Michael Stuart Brown, destined to become a groundbreaking geneticist, was born in 1941. His journey from Brooklyn to scientific stardom led him to win the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine on April 13, 1985, for his revolutionary work in genetics.
activity_question: What prestigious award did Michael Stuart Brown win?

activity_answer: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine

title: Rocky's Musical Mastermind
story: Imagine a world without the iconic Rocky theme. Bill Conti, the musical genius behind it, was born in 1942. On April 13 that year, Providence, Rhode Island welcomed a baby who'd grow up to create unforgettable movie music. Conti's talent led him to compose for films like Rocky and The Right Stuff, earning him both Emmy and Academy Awards for his powerful scores.
activity_question: What famous movie did Bill Conti compose music for?

title: Little Feat's Lowell George: Rock Legend Born
story: A musical star was born in Hollywood on April 13, 1945. Lowell George, the future voice of Little Feat, entered the world ready to rock. He grew up to become a talented singer, songwriter, and guitarist. George created catchy tunes like 'Willin'' and 'Dixie Chicken' that got fans tapping their toes.
activity_question: What famous rock band did Lowell George belong to?
bonus_fact: Lowell George was born in the same city where many movies are made - Hollywood, California!

title: France's Surprise Victory at Bergen
story: Cannons roared and swords clashed as France faced a powerful European alliance. Against the odds, French forces emerged victorious in the Battle of Bergen on April 13, 1759. This clash took place during the Seven Years' War, a global conflict that spread across continents. France's unexpected triumph boosted morale and proved they were still a force to be reckoned with on the battlefield.
activity_question: What was the name of the battle where France defeated a European alliance?
follow_up_question: Why do you think battles were important in shaping history?

title: People Power: Changing How Senators Are Chosen
story: Democracy got a boost in 1911! On April 13, the US House of Representatives made a big decision. They voted to change how senators are picked. Instead of state lawmakers choosing them, the people would now vote directly. This was a huge step towards giving more power to everyday citizens in America's government.
activity_question: What did the US House of Representatives vote to change?
activity_choices: How many senators each state gets
The length of a senator's term
The minimum age to be a senator
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important for people to vote directly for their senators?

title: Heroes Free Dutch Towns from Nazi Control
story: Brave Canadian soldiers charged into action in 1945, freeing two Dutch towns from Nazi control. On April 13, the army rolled into Teuge and Assen, Netherlands, bringing hope to people who had suffered under enemy occupation. The locals cheered as their liberators arrived, marking a big step towards ending World War II in Europe.
activity_question: Which two Dutch towns did Canadian soldiers liberate?
follow_up_question: Why do you think the people in these towns were so happy to see the Canadian soldiers?

title: Alabama's Rainy Day Record
story: Splash! In 1955, the town of Axis, Alabama, got soaked. On April 13, rain poured down non-stop. By the end, over 20 inches of water had fallen from the sky! This massive downpour set a new state record for the most rain in one day. The soggy citizens of Axis probably wished they had bigger umbrellas!
activity_question: How much rain fell in Axis, Alabama?
bonus_fact: This record stood for over 40 years until 1997.
